skill.name: vibe-kanban
skill.description: Manage AI coding agents on a visual Kanban board. Run parallel agents through a To Do to In Progress to Review to Done flow with automatic git worktree isolation and GitHub PR creation.
skill.summary: Visual Kanban board for orchestrating multiple AI coding agents in parallel with git isolation.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 354 patterns across 11 files, but evaluation confirms these are false positives. External command detections are documentation examples in markdown files, not executable code. Network findings are localhost bindings and documentation URLs. Environment variable access is expected for an AI agent orchestration tool requiring API keys. Filesystem access targets legitimate config directories. One elevated concern: docker-compose.yml optionally mounts Docker socket for advanced deployment scenarios, but this is user opt-in.

high_findings.title: Docker Socket Mount in Optional Deployment Template
high_findings.description: The docker-compose.yml deployment template includes a volume mount for /var/run/docker.sock to enable Docker-in-Docker scenarios. This is a known elevated permission pattern that could allow container escape if the container is compromised. This configuration is opt-in for advanced deployments, not the default local development setup.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct volume mount of Docker socket visible in deployment template. This is a documented DevOps pattern but requires security awareness from users who choose this deployment method.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Documentation in Example File
low_findings.description: The .env.example template documents API key formats including placeholder values for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, and GITHUB_TOKEN. This is standard practice for documenting required credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Example file clearly shows placeholder credential formats - standard documentation pattern, not actual secrets.

low_findings.title: Hidden Configuration Directory Access
low_findings.description: Scripts and documentation reference hidden directories like ~/.claude/, ~/.vibe-kanban-workspaces/, and ~/.config/opencode/ for storing configuration and workspace data. These are standard user-level configuration locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: References to dotfiles in home directory are for legitimate configuration storage, following Unix conventions.

content.user_title: Orchestrate AI Agents on a Visual Kanban Board
content.value_statement: Managing multiple AI coding agents leads to git conflicts and lost context. This skill provides visual task tracking with automatic git worktree isolation, enabling parallel agent execution without conflicts.
actual_capabilities: Create visual Kanban board with To Do, In Progress, Review, and Done columns
Run multiple AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Opencode) in parallel with isolated git worktrees
Automatically create git branches and worktrees for each task
Generate GitHub draft pull requests when tasks complete
Stream agent execution logs and display file diffs in browser UI
Support MCP protocol for agent-to-board API integration
limitations: Requires Node.js 18+ and pnpm package manager for local development
Each agent requires valid API keys configured in environment variables
Remote access (VIBE_KANBAN_REMOTE=true) should only be enabled on trusted networks
Docker deployment with socket access requires careful security consideration

use_cases.title: Parallel Epic Implementation
use_cases.description: Break a large feature epic into independent tasks and assign different AI agents to implement frontend, backend, and tests simultaneously. Review all results through the Kanban board before merging.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers managing complex feature implementations

use_cases.title: Team Collaboration with PR Workflow
use_cases.description: Enable remote board access for team visibility while maintaining GitHub PR-based code review. Agents handle implementation, humans handle approval through standard PR process.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams integrating AI agents into existing workflows

use_cases.title: Implementation Comparison Strategy
use_cases.description: Create two workspaces with the same task but different agents (e.g., Claude Code vs Codex). Compare the resulting PRs to select the best approach or combine insights.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads evaluating agent capabilities for specific domains

prompt_templates.title: Basic Task Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the Vibe Kanban board and create a new card. Title it 'Add user authentication endpoint'. In the description, specify: 'Implement POST /api/auth/login with JWT token generation. Use bcrypt for password hashing.' Select Claude Code as the agent and move the card to In Progress to start execution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with single agent task execution

prompt_templates.title: Parallel Agent Assignment
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three tasks for the payment flow epic. Create three cards: (1) 'Payment UI Components' for frontend, (2) 'Payment API Endpoints' for backend, (3) 'Payment E2E Tests' for testing. Assign Claude Code to the UI task, Codex to the API task, and Opencode to the test task. Move all three to In Progress to run in parallel.
prompt_templates.scenario: Decomposing an epic into parallel agent work

prompt_templates.title: Code Review and PR Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all cards in the Review column. For each card, examine the file diffs in the Changes panel and check the agent logs for any warnings. If the implementation looks correct, click 'Open pull request' to create a GitHub draft PR. Move approved cards to the Done column.

prompt_templates.title: MCP-Integrated Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure Vibe Kanban as an MCP server in your agent settings. Then instruct your agent: 'List all cards currently in the In Progress column using the vk_list_cards tool. For any card that has been running for more than 10 minutes, use vk_get_logs to check if the agent is stuck.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Using MCP tools for automated monitoring

output_examples.input: Create a card for implementing user profile page
output_examples.output: Card created: 'Implement user profile page' (ID: card_abc123) in To Do column. Select an agent and move to In Progress to begin execution.

output_examples.input: Move card to In Progress with Claude Code agent
output_examples.output: Git worktree created at ~/.vibe-kanban-workspaces/ws-uuid-123. Branch vk/a1b2-user-profile created from main. Claude Code agent started with task description. Logs streaming in real-time.

output_examples.input: Review completed card and create PR
output_examples.output: PR #42 created as draft: 'Add user profile page' (vk/a1b2-user-profile). Changes: 3 files modified (src/pages/profile.tsx, src/api/profile.ts, src/styles/profile.css). Card moved to Done column.

best_practices: Keep each card scope narrow - one card should represent one commit unit or feature slice
For tasks spanning multiple files, review the implementation plan with a planning tool before starting agent execution
Enable VIBE_KANBAN_REMOTE only on trusted networks and always use VK_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to restrict CORS
anti_patterns: Creating cards with broad scope like 'Refactor entire backend' - split into smaller, independent tasks
Running agents without git worktree isolation - always use the board to manage agent execution context
Leaving orphaned worktrees after failed executions - run 'git worktree prune' periodically to clean up

faq.question: What happens if an agent gets stuck or fails mid-execution?
faq.answer: The card moves to 'Needs Attention' state. You can view logs to diagnose the issue, then use the 'Retry' button to re-run the agent. Optionally clear the worktree to start fresh.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without the browser UI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes CLI scripts (scripts/conductor.sh, scripts/pipeline.sh) for automation scenarios. MCP tools also allow programmatic board control via vk_* API calls.

faq.question: How does git worktree isolation prevent conflicts?
faq.answer: Each workspace gets its own checkout of the repository in a separate directory with an isolated branch. Agents work in parallel without modifying the same files, and PRs are merged individually.

faq.question: Do I need to configure API keys for every agent I want to use?
faq.answer: Yes. Each agent (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, etc.) requires its respective API key in environment variables. Configure them in the Settings → Agents panel or via .env file before starting.

faq.question: Is the Docker socket mount required for local development?
faq.answer: No. The Docker socket volume in docker-compose.yml is only for advanced deployment scenarios requiring Docker-in-Docker. Local development uses npx or pnpm directly without Docker.

faq.question: Can multiple team members access the same Kanban board?
faq.answer: Yes, with VIBE_KANBAN_REMOTE=true and VK_ALLOWED_ORIGINS configured. Team members can view board status remotely, but code review and approval still happen through GitHub PRs.
